SEARCH
Package com.tngtech.archunit.core.domain

Interface JavaParameterizedType

All Superinterfaces:
HasName, JavaType
@PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public interface JavaParameterizedType extends JavaType
A JavaParameterizedType represents a concrete parameterization of a generic type. Consider the generic type List<T>, then List<String> would be a parameterized type where the concrete type java.lang.String has been assigned to the type variable T. The concrete type java.lang.String is then an "actual type argument" of this JavaParameterizedType (see getActualTypeArguments()).

  • Method Details

    • getActualTypeArguments

      @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) List<JavaType> getActualTypeArguments()
      Returns:
      The actual type arguments of this parameterized type (compare JavaParameterizedType).

    • traverseSignature

      default void traverseSignature(JavaType.SignatureVisitor visitor)
      Description copied from interface: JavaType
      Traverses through the signature of this JavaType.
      This method considers the type signature as a tree, where e.g. a JavaClass is a simple leaf, but a JavaParameterizedType has the type as root and then branches out into its actual type arguments, which in turn can have type arguments or upper/lower bounds in case of JavaTypeVariable or JavaWildcardType.
      The following is a simple visualization of such a signature tree: 
      
 List<Map<? extends Serializable, String[]>>
                    |
    Map<? extends Serializable, String[]>
              /                   \
  ? extends Serializable         String[]
            |
      Serializable
      For every node visited the respective method of the provided visitor will be invoked. The traversal happens depth first, i.e. in this case the visitor would be invoked for all types down to Serializable before visiting the String[] array type of the second branch. At every step it is possible to continue the traversal by returning CONTINUE or stop at that point by returning STOP.

      Note that the traversal will continue to traverse bounds of type variables, even if that type variable isn't declared in this signature itself.
      E.g. take the following scenario 
      
 class Example<T extends String> {
     T field;
 }
      Traversing the field type of field will continue down to the upper bounds of the type variable T and thus end at the type String.

      Also, note that the traversal will not continue down the type parameters of a raw type declared in a signature.
      E.g. given the signature class Example<T extends Map> the traversal would stop at Map and not traverse down the type parameters K and V of Map.
      Specified by:
      traverseSignature in interface JavaType
      Parameters:
      visitor - A JavaType.SignatureVisitor to invoke for every encountered JavaType while traversing this signature.
